title: Antislop: A Comprehensive Framework for Identifying and Eliminating Repetitive Patterns in Language Models
author_or_org: Samuel Paech, Allen Roush, Judah Goldfeder, Ravid Shwartz-Ziv
supported_claim: Repetitive LLM phraseology is measurable and has motivated a detection, sampling, data-generation, and fine-tuning framework.
measured_effort: Framework and released code evaluate 8,000+ patterns; paper reports cross-domain experiments.

title: NousResearch autonovel
supported_claim: A production-oriented content pipeline embeds mechanical anti-slop rules, separate LLM judging, and repeated review/revision.
measured_effort: Repository documents 27 Python tools, 6 automated revision cycles, 6 review rounds, and a 79,456-word output.

title: peakoss/anti-slop
source_type: primary source repository and GitHub Action
supported_claim: Maintainers have built automated controls to reduce low-quality and AI-generated pull-request review load.
measured_effort: 34 checks, 57 configuration options, rules derived from 130+ manually reviewed PRs, and reported experience with 120+ slop PRs per month.

title: Stop Slop
supported_claim: Writers use explicit phrase, structure, sentence, scoring, and rewrite controls to remove recurring AI-writing patterns.
measured_effort: Repository contains a core skill, three reference collections, examples, scoring rules, changelog, and 12 recorded commits at access time.

title: De-AI-ify scientific writing
supported_claim: Scientific-writing workflows use a specialized rubric to detect formulaic structures, filler, false agency, fragmentation, and vague claims.
measured_effort: Dedicated installable skill and reference set were created for scientific and technical prose.

title: Detecting AI-Generated Content in Academic Peer Reviews
supported_claim: Large-scale scholarly review workflows face a growing volume of AI-assisted content and associated integrity, reliability, and transparency questions.
measured_effort: Study trains and applies a detector across historical ICLR and Nature Communications review collections through 2025.

title: What happens when reviewers receive AI feedback in their reviews?
author_or_org: Shiping Chen, Shu Zhong, Duncan P. Brumby, Anna L. Cox
supported_claim: A live ICLR review process deployed AI feedback and required study of usability, quality, agency, fairness, accountability, and trust.
measured_effort: Live ICLR 2025 deployment studied through surveys and interviews.

title: Wikipedia:WikiProject AI Cleanup
supported_claim: Editors maintain a dedicated project, guidance, queues, and cleanup processes for low-quality AI-generated encyclopedia content.
measured_effort: Dedicated WikiProject and maintained cleanup/list-of-signs workflow; current revision dated 2026-07-10.
